items.title: Deterministic Node Execution and Transition Provenance
Hermetic and Non-Destructive Delivery Gate
Versioning, changelog and upgrade impact governance
Playbook Versioning and Runtime Upgrade Handoff
items.summary: Introduce explicit playbook versions, concise per-version change records, compatibility metadata and a machine-readable upgrade handoff so an already-running process can determine what changed and whether migration or revalidation is required.
acceptance_criteria: Every canonical playbook declares a semantic version and the language/framework versions used to build it.
Every playbook release contains a concise machine-readable and human-readable changelog.
Backlog items completed for a playbook release are linked to that playbook version.
A running process can compare its active playbook version with a newly supplied version and receive no_action, recommended_revalidation, migration_required or upgrade_blocked.
The handoff identifies changed nodes, contracts, outputs, gates and migration instructions without automatically mutating active runtime state.
Playbook packages include provenance, compatibility ranges, previous-version reference and rollback metadata.
Validation, migration, negative and backward-compatibility tests pass.

items.title: Test Fixture Mutation and Parallel Execution Safety
items.summary: Prevent silent negative-fixture no-ops, classify shared-state tests, serialize workspace-mutating and wall-clock-sensitive tests, and enforce deterministic release-gate scheduling.
acceptance_criteria: SILENT_FIXTURE_NOOP is a blocking registered anti-pattern.
Negative fixture mutation proves that the fixture changed.
Test files have machine-readable execution-safety classes.
Workspace-mutating and performance-sensitive tests do not run under parallel contention.
Safety audit, regression tests, package lints and non-destructive gate pass.

items.title: Canonical Archive Identity and Evidence Freshness
items.summary: Guarantee archive filename/internal-root identity, immutable post-checksum trees, current-run evidence and release/version consistency during canonical packaging.
acceptance_criteria: Archive filename, internal root and release ID derive from one manifest.
SHA256SUMS is generated last and any later mutation blocks release.
Gate reports prove current run ID, source tree hash and timestamp.
Post-unpack verification checks identity, freshness and version consistency.

scope: state diff, transition and evidence linkage

items.title: Journey Replay, Cross-Version Migration and Automated Comparison
Playbook Version Rollback Checkpoint Governance
Empirical Evidence Subsystem Foundation
items.goal: Introduce an independent but interoperable canonical empirical-evidence subsystem with machine-readable contracts, validation, provenance, admission governance, and strict separation of raw evidence, derived measurements, findings, and comparative claims.
required_scope: Create the canonical empirical_evidence top-level subsystem structure.
Define schemas for case, run, finding, scorecard, claim, admission, task class, and evidence envelope.
Add evidence indexes, provenance rules, contamination states, and immutable raw-evidence handling.
Add a validator and canonical package integration rules.
Keep the subsystem independent from CSG and production-readiness gates.

items.title: Empirical Evidence Base v1.3 Canonical Migration
items.goal: Migrate ORDO_EMPIRICAL_EVIDENCE_BASE_v1_3 into the canonical empirical-evidence subsystem without altering raw evidence, historical scores, or bounded conclusions.
required_scope: Verify and preserve all source hashes and raw run archives.
Map TC01/EX01 into the new schemas and indexes.
Separate raw evidence, derived scorecards, findings, and claims.
Record limitations, contamination decisions, and admission status.
Produce migration and post-migration validation reports.

items.title: Empirical Evidence and Existing Evaluation Interoperability
items.goal: Add a non-merging interoperability bridge between the empirical-evidence subsystem and the existing CSG/evaluation mechanisms.
required_scope: Define a shared evidence envelope and stable cross-system identifiers.
Link playbook, model, scenario, run, language, framework, evaluator, and raw-archive identities.
Allow CSG evidence to be referenced by empirical cases without changing either scoring system.
Prevent empirical comparison claims from automatically changing production-readiness gates.
Document independent lifecycle, ownership, and compatibility rules.

items.title: Multi-Task-Class Evidence Expansion and Scoring Calibration
items.goal: Validate the empirical-evidence subsystem across additional task classes and calibrate task-class-specific scoring and reproducibility profiles.
required_scope: Add canonical cases for TC02 and TC03 or equivalent additional task classes.
Define repeated-run and model-configuration reproducibility contracts.
Evaluate whether scoring weights require task-class-specific profiles.
Track supporting, contradicting, and retired claims.
Do not generalize Ordo advantage beyond admitted evidence.

items.title: Publication-Safe External Developer Adoption Evidence Preparation
items.goal: Transform the first independent external-developer playbook creation record into a publication-safe English evidence package while preserving process, timing, testing, rollback, and provenance evidence.

items.title: Canonical Integration of EDA-001 External Developer Adoption Evidence
items.goal: Integrate EDA-001 as the first canonical external-developer adoption evidence case, validate its publication-safe package and provenance, and release it through the full canonical delivery gate.

items.title: Prompt-Only Compilation Target and Evidence-Based Runtime Routing
items.goal: Add a new Ordo playbook compilation target that converts a canonical Ordo source playbook into a lightweight language-instruction package for direct model use, while preserving the Ordo source as the single source of truth and explicitly declaring which runtime guarantees are retained or lost.

concept.routing_principle: Use empirical evidence to decide whether a task may safely run as a compiled prompt-only package or must execute through the full Ordo engine/runtime.

required_scope: Define `prompt_only` as a first-class compilation/package profile.
Compile intent, contracts, nodes, branches, gates, state rules, artifacts, correction, backtrack, and completion behavior into a coherent model-instruction package.
Create a mandatory compilation manifest linked to the exact Ordo source SHA-256.
Declare `guarantees_retained` and `guarantees_lost`; fail packaging if this declaration is absent or incomplete.
Prevent compiled prompt artifacts from claiming full Ordo runtime guarantees.
Bind every compiled package to the exact source version, language version, framework version, compiler version, and model/evaluation profile.
Invalidate compiled output automatically whenever the Ordo source or compilation policy changes.
Define evidence-based routing thresholds between `prompt_only` and `engine_runtime`.
Use existing CSG/evaluation and empirical-evidence mechanisms without replacing or prematurely merging them.
Add periodic revalidation against model/provider/version drift.
Automatically recommend or require escalation to `engine_runtime` when branch, backtrack, state, gate, or adversarial-noise quality falls below threshold.
Add positive, negative, confusion, branch, backtrack, skipped-step, correction, and model-drift tests.
Document when prompt-only execution is appropriate and when it is prohibited.
acceptance_criteria: The same canonical Ordo source can produce both engine-runtime and prompt-only artifacts.
Prompt-only output is deterministic for a fixed compiler/version/configuration where feasible.
The output package contains an explicit, machine-readable guarantee-loss declaration.
No prompt-only package may be labeled equivalent to full Ordo runtime execution.
Compiled artifacts are invalidated by source/version/compiler drift.
A reproducible evaluation route determines whether prompt-only remains acceptable.
The system can escalate from prompt-only to engine-runtime based on measured degradation.
Canonical tests and post-unpack verification pass.
out_of_scope_for_now: Automatic migration of all current playbooks.
items.activation_note: When this task is taken into work, first split it into compiler/profile, manifest/binding, evaluation routing, model-drift revalidation, migration, and conformance-test workstreams.
items.closure: Implemented prompt-only compilation target, manifest/binding, evidence-based routing, model-drift revalidation rules, tests, CLI, documentation, and book chapters.

items.title: ARF Delivery Target Decision Gate
items.goal: Integrate an analyst-confirmed delivery target decision into the ARF authoring flow after canonical playbook stabilization.
required_scope: Recommend engine_runtime, prompt_only, or both from risk, complexity, enforcement needs, and evidence.
Ask the analyst only after the canonical contract and validation are stable.
Show prompt-only guarantee losses before confirmation.
Use engine_runtime as the safe default.
Generate target-specific handoff package requirements.
Add CLI, schema, tests, documentation, and book chapters.

items.scope_note: Separate from the delivery-target decision gate; no silent scope expansion.
